name: Waveshare E-Paper Display
description: The `waveshare_epaper` display platform allows you to use some E-Paper displays sold by [Waveshare](https://www.waveshare.com/product/displays/e-paper.htm) with ESPHome. The 2.13" [TTGO module](https://github.com/lewisxhe/TTGO-EPaper-Series) and the [Waveshare Cloud Module](https://www.waveshare.com/wiki/2.13inch_e-Paper_Cloud_Module) with an ESP32 on the board are supported as well. Depending on your specific revision of the TTGO board you might need to try out the `-b73` or `-b1` version (see below). The 1.54" [Good Display gdew0154m09](https://www.good-display.com/product/206.html) as used in the [M5Stack Core Ink](https://shop.m5stack.com/products/m5stack-esp32-core-ink-development-kit1-54-elnk-display) is also supported. Similar modules sold by other vendors might also work but not have been tested yet.

config_entries.label: SPI ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID of the [SPI Component](https://esphome.io/components/spi) if you want to use multiple SPI buses.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to re-draw the screen. Defaults to `1s`, use `never` to only manually update the screen via `component.update`.

config_entries.label: Model
config_entries.description: The model of the E-Paper display. Options are:

options.label: 2.13in V2 display (Pico e-Paper 2.13v2 and Cloud Module)
T5_V2.3 tested. Also works for Wemos D1 Mini ePaper Shield 2.13 1.0.0 "LOLIN"
GooDisplay GDEW029T5, as used on the AdaFruit MagTag and Pimoroni Badger
currently not working with the HAT Rev 2.1 version
GooDisplay GDEY029t94, as used in the monochrome 2.9inch display from seeedstudio
2.9in V2 display, but with different initialization and full/partial display refresh management than `2.90inv2`
7.3in 7-color display (black, white, red, yellow, blue, green, and orange)
display with version sticker '(C)' on the back, B/W rendering only
Can't use with an ESP8266 as it runs out of RAM
T5_V2.3 with DKE group display (DEPG0213BN) tested
GoodDisplay gdew0154m09, as used in the M5Stack Core Ink
13.3in, with the K model, 960x680, B/W rendering only

config_entries.label: Busy Pin
config_entries.description: The BUSY pin. Defaults to not connected.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: The CS pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.description: The DC pin.

config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description:
**Set at most one of:** `pages`, `lambda`.

The lambda to use for rendering the content on the display. See [Display Rendering Engine](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-engine) for more information.

config_entries.label: Pages
config_entries.description:
**Set at most one of:** `pages`, `lambda`.

Show pages instead of a single lambda. See [Display Pages](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-pages).

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: The RESET pin. Defaults to not connected. Make sure you pull this pin high (by connecting it to 3.3V with a resistor) if not connected to a GPIO pin. The 2.13" B74 and V2 display variants require the reset pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Auto Clear Enabled
config_entries.description: If the display should be cleared before each update. Defaults to `true` if a lambda or pages are configured, false otherwise.

config_entries.label: Full Update Every
config_entries.description: E-Paper displays have two modes of switching to the next image: A partial update that only changes the pixels that have changed and a full update mode that first clears the entire display and then re-draws the image. The former is much quicker and nicer, but every so often a full update needs to happen because artifacts accumulate. On the `1.54in`, `1.54inv2`, `2.13in`, `2.13inv2`, `2.90in`, `2.90inv2`, `7.50inV2p` and `gdew029t5` models, you have the option to only do a full-redraw every x-th time using this option. Defaults to `30` on the described models and a full update for all other models.

config_entries.label: Reset Duration
config_entries.description: Duration for the display reset operation. Defaults to `200ms`. Setting this value to `2ms` may resolve issues with newer e-Paper Driver modules (e.g. Rev 2.1).

config_entries.label: Rotation
config_entries.description: Set the rotation of the display. Everything you draw in `lambda:` will be rotated by this option. One of `0°` (default), `90°`, `180°`, `270°`.
